DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SENSORIA, LLC'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer to causes of action one through four are sustained without leave to amend. Those claims sound in negligence and the exception to worker's compensation exclusivity under Labor 3602(b)(1) ["a willful physical assault by the employer"] is inapplicable to these claims. (See Coit Drapery Cleaners. Inc. v. Sequoia Ins. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 1595, 1606 [worker's compensation bars negligence-based claims].)

Plaintiff has 15-days leave to amend to plead causes of action for assault and/or battery against defendant. If plaintiff can do so in good faith, plaintiff has leave to allege that defendant ratified these intentional torts and is therefore liable as a joint participant under the doctrine of ratification. (See Fretland v. County of Humboldt (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1478, 1489-1490.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
